# ☁️ CLOUD WEEKLY

## **Agentic AI Takes the Wheel: AWS Goes All-In on Autonomous Assistants**

---

### EDITOR'S NOTE

This week, the cloud landscape shifted decisively toward agentic AI—systems that don't just respond, but *act*. AWS dropped major announcements that signal we're moving from "AI as a feature" to "AI as a coworker," and the infrastructure to support it is maturing fast.

---

### 🔥 TOP STORY

**AWS Unveils Amazon Quick and Expands Agentic AI Solutions**

At the "What's Next with AWS" 2026 event, AWS launched **Amazon Quick**—a new AI assistant designed for enterprise work with a dedicated desktop app and deep integrations across the AWS ecosystem. But the bigger play? AWS expanded **Amazon Connect** into four specialized agentic AI solutions targeting supply chain, hiring, customer experience, and healthcare. This isn't just AI answering questions—it's AI executing workflows end-to-end.

[Read more →](https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/aws/top-announcements-of-the-whats-next-with-aws-2026/)

---

### ⚡ QUICK HITS

- **Claude Opus 4.7 lands in Bedrock** with a massive 1M token context window and improved agentic coding capabilities—your AI pair programmer just got a serious memory upgrade.

- **AWS Interconnect hits GA** with multicloud private connectivity, making hybrid and multi-cloud architectures significantly cleaner to implement.

- **Parallel Web Systems**, the AI agent startup from ex-Twitter CEO Parag Agrawal, just hit a $2B valuation after raising another $100M from Sequoia.

- **Microsoft's cloud business keeps climbing** even as Xbox hardware revenue dropped 33%—proof that enterprise cloud remains the real growth engine.

---

### 🤖 THIS WEEK IN AI

The agentic AI race is accelerating. Beyond AWS's moves, **Runway's CEO** is already looking past AI video to "world models"—AI systems that understand and simulate entire environments. Meanwhile, the Anthropic & Meta partnership mentioned in AWS's weekly roundup hints at interesting model collaboration ahead. The **Bedrock AgentCore CLI** launch also gives engineers new tools to build and deploy AI agents faster. Bottom line: if you're not experimenting with agentic architectures, you're already behind.

---

### 💡 CLOSING THOUGHT

**This week's action item:** Spin up a proof-of-concept with Amazon Bedrock's AgentCore
